---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0doc-extract-contracts
key: sv0doc-extract-contracts
state: done
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0doc
    - contracts
title: "extract contract system semantics"
type: task
---

# extract contract system semantics

## purpose

extract the contract system semantics from the sv0 design document into a
formal specification at `sv0doc/contracts/semantics.md`. contracts are the
defining feature of sv0 -- this specification must be precise enough to
implement the contract analyzer in sv0c.

## dependencies

/require: bash

## configuration

/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc
/env: CONTRACTS_FILE=$SV0DOC_ROOT/contracts/semantics.md

## extraction

/ai: extract the complete contract system semantics from the sv0 design document.

     organize into these sections:

     1. contract syntax
        - requires(expr) -- preconditions on function entry
        - ensures(expr) -- postconditions on function exit
        - loop_invariant(expr) -- loop invariants
        - placement: between function signature and body
        - multiple contract clauses (additive conjunction)

     2. contract builtins
        - result: binding to the return value in ensures clauses
          - direct binding for non-Result return types
          - pattern matching with match for Result<T,E> and Option<T>
          - tuple access (result.0, result.1) for tuple returns
        - old(expr): pre-state snapshot at function entry
          - valid only in ensures clauses
          - compiler generates temporaries for runtime checking
        - forall(var in range, predicate): universal quantifier
          - runtime: iterates over range
          - static: SMT solver proves
        - exists(var in range, predicate): existential quantifier
        - no_alias(a, b): asserts two references don't alias
        - borrows(x, y): declares borrow sources for returned references

     3. contract verification phases
        - phase 1 (runtime): all contracts compiled to runtime checks
          - failed check: prints source location, expression, variable values, aborts
        - phase 2 (local static): SMT solver proves contracts within a function
          - proven contracts produce no runtime code
          - unproven contracts remain as runtime checks
          - sv0 verify reports verification status
        - phase 3 (modular static): cross-function verification
          - caller's arguments proven against callee's requires
          - refinement types checked at assignment

     4. contract-mode settings
        - runtime: all contracts are runtime checks (default, phase 1+)
        - verified: proven contracts stripped, unproven remain (phase 2+)
        - disabled: all contracts stripped (phase 1+, release builds)

     5. trait contracts
        - trait methods can have contracts
        - impl must satisfy trait contracts
        - impl can strengthen with additional contracts
        - contravariance: impl requires can be weaker, ensures can be stronger

     6. narrowing cast contracts
        - `x as u8` implicitly inserts requires(x <= 255)
        - compiler proves or inserts runtime check
        - .truncate() for intentional wrapping (no contract)
        - .try_into() for checked conversion returning Result

     write the complete specification to sv0doc/contracts/semantics.md.

/run: 01-validate-contracts.sh

## validation

/assert: test -f $CONTRACTS_FILE
/assert: test -s $CONTRACTS_FILE
